author: Augustine of Hippo

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XV. 8–10.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Saviour, in thus speaking to the disciples, commends still more and more the grace whereby we are saved, when He says, “Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear very much fruit, and be made my disciples.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whether we say glorified, or made bright, both are the rendering given us of one Greek verb, namely doxazein (δοξάζειν).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is doxa (δόξα) in Greek, is in Latin glory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have thought it worth while to mention this, because the apostle says, “If Abraham was justified by works, he hath glory, but not before God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is the glory before God, whereby God, and not man, is glorified, when he is justified, not by works, but by faith, so that even his doing well is imparted to him by God; just as the branch, as I have stated above, cannot bear fruit of itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if herein God the Father is glorified, that we bear much fruit, and be made the disciples of Christ, let us not credit our own glory therewith, as if we had it of ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For of Him is such a grace, and accordingly therein the glory is not ours, but His.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence also, in another passage, after saying, “Let your light so shine before men that they may see your good works;” to keep them from the thought that such good works were of themselves, He immediately added, “and may glorify your Father who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For herein is the Father glorified, that we bear much fruit, and be made the disciples of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And by whom are we so made, but by Him whose mercy hath forestalled us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As the Father hath loved me,” He says, “so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, then, you see, is the source of our good works.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For whence should we have them, were it not that faith worketh by love?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how should we love, were it not that we were first loved?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With striking clearness is this declared by the same evangelist in his epistle: “We love God because He first loved us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when He says, “As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you,” He indicates no such equality between our nature and His as there is between Himself and the Father, but the grace whereby the Mediator between God and men is the man Christ Jesus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He is pointed out as Mediator when He says, “The Father—me, and I—you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father, indeed, also loveth us, but in Him; for herein is the Father glorified, that we bear fruit in the vine, that is, in the Son, and so be made His disciples.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Continue ye,” He says, “in my love.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How shall we continue?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to what follows: “If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Love brings about the keeping of His commandments; but does the keeping of His commandments bring about love?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who can doubt that it is love which precedes?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he has no true ground for keeping the commandments who is destitute of love.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, in saying, “If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love,” He shows not the source from which love springs, but the means whereby it is manifested.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if He said, Think not that ye abide in my love if ye keep not my commandments; for it is only if ye have kept them that ye shall abide.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In other words, it will thus be made apparent that ye shall abide in my love if ye keep my commandments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So that no one need deceive himself by saying that he loveth Him, if he keepeth not His commandments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we love Him just in the same measure as we keep His commandments; and the less we keep them, the less we love.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And although, when He saith, “Continue ye in my love,” it is not apparent what love He spake of; whether the love we bear to Him, or that which He bears to us: yet it is seen at once in the previous clause.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He had there said, “So have I loved you;” and to these words He immediately adds, “Continue ye in my love:” accordingly, it is that love which He bears to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, do the words mean, “Continue ye in my love,” but just, continue ye in my grace?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what do these mean, “If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love,” but, hereby shall ye know that ye shall abide in the love which I bear to you, if ye keep my commandments?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not, then, for the purpose of awakening His love to us that we first keep His commandments; but this, that unless He loves us, we cannot keep His commandments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is a grace which lies all disclosed to the humble, but is hid from the proud.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what are we to make of that which follows: “Even as I have kept my Father’s commandments, and abide in His love”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here also He certainly intended us to understand that fatherly love wherewith He was loved of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this was what He has just said, “As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you;” and then to these He added the words, “Continue ye in my love;” in that, doubtless, wherewith I have loved you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, when He says also of the Father, “I abide in His love,” we are to understand it of that love which was borne Him by the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But then, in this case also, is that love which the Father bears to the Son referable to the same grace as that wherewith we are loved of the Son: seeing that we on our part are sons, not by nature, but by grace; while the Only-begotten is so by nature and not by grace?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or is this even in the Son Himself to be referred to His condition as man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in saying, “As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you,” He pointed to the grace that was His as Mediator.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ Jesus is the Mediator between God and men, not in respect to His Godhead, but in respect to His manhood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And certainly it is in reference to this His human nature that we read, “And Jesus increased in wisdom and age, and in favor [grace] with God and men.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In harmony, therefore, with this, we may rightly say that while human nature belongs not to the nature of God, yet such human nature does by grace belong to the person of the only-begotten Son of God; and that by grace so great, that there is none greater, yea, none that even approaches equality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there were no merits that preceded that assumption of humanity, but all His merits began with that very assumption.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son, therefore, abideth in the love wherewith the Father hath loved Him, and so hath kept His commandments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what are we to think of Him even as man, but that God is His lifter up? for the Word was God, the Only-begotten, co-eternal with Him that begat; but that He might be given to us as Mediator, by grace ineffable, the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.
